column1: Advorix Technologies Business Insights Report

For the following task, Airline ticketing data has been taken into consideration. The Airlines that have been taken are the following: Air India, British Airways, Emirates, Etihad, Lufthansa, Qatar Airways, Singapore Airlines and Thai Airways.

The data comparison is happening across the different airline classes namely business, economy and first. The fields taken into consideration for the comparison are the number of tickets sold and the revenue generated by the sales.
1.ĀĀ Air India
In terms of number of tickets sold, Economy class takes the lead with 1450 tickets closely followed by First class with 1257 tickets and the least being Business class with 727 tickets.

In terms of revenue as well Economy class takes the lead followed by First class and the least being Business class again. This indicates that the number of tickets sold multiplied by the unit price per ticket is directly proportionate to the revenue generated.
From Air India data we can conclude that Economy class is their best seller and Business class is their least seller.
2.ĀĀ British Airways
In terms of number of tickets sold Economy class takes the lead with 1530 units followed by First class with 1323 and Business class with 871.

In terms of revenue generated however First class takes the lead followed by Economy class and Business class at the last. The reason for this is that the unit price for the First class tickets is higher than the unit price for the Economy class tickets. Hence despite having more units sold, revenue generated by First class is greater than revenue generated by Economy class.
3.ĀĀ Emirates
In terms of number of tickets sold, Economy class has the most sales with 1529 units sold followed by First class at 1011 units sold and finally Business class with 520 units sold.

In terms of revenue generated Economy class takes the lead followed by First class and then at the last is Business class. From this we can understand that Economy class is the most popular or preferred and Business class is the least popular or preferred.
4.ĀĀ Etihad
In terms of number of tickets sold Business class takes the lead with 1418 units sold. This is followed by First class at 638 tickets sold and Economy at the last with 394 units sold.

In terms of revenue generation Business class takes the lead followed by First class and at the last place is Economy class. From this we can understand that Business class is the most favoured whereas Economy class is the least favoured.
5.ĀĀ Lufthansa

In terms of tickets sold, Economy class has the greatest number of tickets sold with 1221 units sold. This is followed by Business class with 1000 units sold and at the last is First class with 676 units sold.

In terms of revenue generation, Business class has generated the most revenue followed by Economy class and First class is the least. From this we can gather that Business class has unit price higher than Economy class due to which revenue generated by Business class tickets sales is higher.
6.ĀĀ Qatar Airways
In terms of number of tickets sold, First class takes the lead with 1500 units followed by Business class with 1057 units and finally Economy class at the last with 817 units sold.

In terms of revenue generated, First class is at the lead followed by Economy class and finally Business class at the last. From this we can understand that Economy class tickets per unit price is higher than that of Business class which is why it is placed second.
7.ĀĀ Singapore Airlines
In terms of ticket sales, Economy class is the most with 1165 units sold. This is followed by Business class at 877 units sold and finally First class with 863 units sold.

In terms of revenue generated, First class is at first position followed by Economy class with Business class being at the last. This is again due to the fact that First class tickets per unit pricing is way higher than that of Economy class and Business class due to which it has generated the highest revenue.
8.ĀĀ Thai Airways
In terms of number of tickets sold, First class takes the lead with 1244 units sold. This is followed by Economy class having sold 1073 units and finally Business class having sold 1014 units.

In terms of revenue generation, First class takes the lead followed by Economy class and at the last is Business class. This makes it evident that First class is the most sold and Business class is the least sold.
Overall Summary across Airlines and Classes

In terms of overall comparison across classes, Etihad has sold the most number of Business class tickets with 1418 in terms of number of units sold. Emirates has sold the least number of Business class tickets at 520 units.
Similarly for Economy class tickets British Airways has sold the maximum number of them with 1530 units sold. Etihad has sold the least number of Economy class tickets with 394 units sold.
In terms of First class tickets, Qatar Airways has sold the greatest number of them with 1500 units sold. Etihad has sold the least number of First class tickets with 638 units sold.
In terms of Overall comparison across the Classes and Airlines, we can see the following data:
British Airways has sold the greatest number of tickets with 3724 units sold
Etihad has sold the least number of tickets with 2450 units sold
Qatar Airways has generated the greatest overall revenue amongst all the airlines
Singapore Airlines has generated the least overall revenue amongst all the airlines
Insights and Conclusion
Conclusion
a)ĀĀ The revenue generated is dependent on the per unit price of the ticket and the number of tickets sold

b)Ā Even if number of tickets sold is high, revenue generated depends on the price of the single unit of it. Hence even if another class ticket has sold lesser number of tickets if its per unit price is higher, the revenue generated by it is higher than that generated by the highest selling unitÆs category.
c)ĀĀĀ The more in advance the tickets are booked the better the fare offered by the airline.
d)Ā All airlines fly across similar and different destinations both but there is price difference based on the airline
Insights

a)ĀĀ Across most airlines Business class isnÆt faring that well compared to Economy and First class. This must be taken into account and appropriate action to be taken through marketing and promotions
b)Ā Customers must be encouraged to book well in advance so that the fares are set accordingly
c)ĀĀĀ Some airlines have good ticketing volume but are trailing behind with revenue generation. Suitable steps must be taken to make revenue generation from them more stable
d)Ā Airlines having good revenue but weak ticketing must take a closer look at improving ticketing sales to further improve and increase revenue generation.
e)ĀĀ First class and Economy class have strong ticketing and revenue generation base. That must be maintained at the same level and with time should also see strategies to improve on it.
